# content-disposition

[![NPM Version][npm-version-image]][npm-url]
[![NPM Downloads][npm-downloads-image]][npm-url]
[![Node.js Version][node-version-image]][node-version-url]
[![Build Status][travis-image]][travis-url]
[![Test Coverage][coveralls-image]][coveralls-url]

Create and parse Content-Disposition header

## Installation

```sh
$ npm install content-disposition
```

## API

```js
var contentDisposition = require('content-disposition')
```

### contentDisposition(filename, options)

Create a Content-Disposition header string using the given `filename`. The
`filename` can be a string or an object with a `.toString()` method such as a
`Buffer`. The `filename` is optional and if no filename is provided, the
returned Content-Disposition header string will not include a `filename` parameter.

```js
contentDisposition('foo.png')
// => "attachment; filename=\"foo.png\""

contentDisposition('foo.png', { type: 'inline' })
// => "inline; filename=\"foo.png\""
```

#### Options

`contentDisposition` accepts these properties in the options object.

##### fallback

An optional fallback filename that will be added as the `filename*` parameter
if the given `filename` contains non-ASCII characters. This should be a string
or an object with a `.toString()` method such as a `Buffer`.

**Note:** The `filename` option should still be passed as the primary name; the
`fallback` is only used as a fallback for clients that do not support the
`filename*` parameter.

##### type

Specify the disposition type. This can be `'attachment'` (default) or `'inline'`.
This can also be a string that includes the `type` and any valid parameters for
the header.

```js
contentDisposition('foo.png', { type: 'inline' })
// => "inline; filename=\"foo.png\""

contentDisposition('foo.png', { type: 'attachment' })
// => "attachment; filename=\"foo.png\""
```

### contentDisposition.parse(string)

Parse a Content-Disposition header string. This automatically handles extended
Content-Disposition headers with the `filename*` parameter (currently only the
UTF-8 encoding is supported).

```js
contentDisposition.parse('attachment; filename="EURO rates.txt"; filename*=UTF-8\'en\'EURO%20rates.txt')
// => { type: 'attachment',
//      parameters: { filename: 'EURO rates.txt' } }
```

## Examples

### Responding with an attachment

```js
var contentDisposition = require('content-disposition')
var express = require('express')

var app = express()

app.get('/attachment', function (req, res) {
  res.setHeader('Content-Disposition', contentDisposition('report.pdf'))
  res.send('This is a PDF report')
})
```

### Responding with an inline file

```js
var contentDisposition = require('content-disposition')
var express = require('express')
var fs = require('fs')

var app = express()

app.get('/image', function (req, res) {
  res.setHeader('Content-Disposition', contentDisposition('landscape.jpg', { type: 'inline' }))
  res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'image/jpeg')
  fs.createReadStream('landscape.jpg').pipe(res)
})
```

### Parsing a header value

```js
var contentDisposition = require('content-disposition')

var header = contentDisposition.parse('attachment; filename="EURO rates.txt"; filename*=UTF-8\'en\'EURO%20rates.txt')

console.log(header.type)  // => 'attachment'
console.log(header.parameters.filename)  // => 'EURO rates.txt'
```

## Usage in Express.js

The content-disposition package is used internally by Express.js to handle file downloads and attachments. When you use `res.download()`, `res.attachment()`, or `res.sendFile()` in Express.js, this package is used to generate the appropriate Content-Disposition header.

```js
var express = require('express')
var app = express()

// Using res.download() - sets attachment Content-Disposition header
app.get('/download', function(req, res) {
  res.download('/path/to/file.pdf', 'report.pdf')
})

// Using res.attachment() - sets attachment Content-Disposition header without sending a file
app.get('/attachment', function(req, res) {
  res.attachment('report.pdf')
  res.send('This is a PDF report')
})

// Using res.sendFile() with attachment option
app.get('/sendfile', function(req, res) {
  res.sendFile('/path/to/file.pdf', {
    headers: {
      'Content-Disposition': 'attachment; filename="custom-name.pdf"'
    }
  })
})
```
